hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.ar: 1343  - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لَّى ذَّإن  َّي ِهِ .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Abu Basir that He said: I heard Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} say,  
"If you are in a place of danger and fear a thief or a wild beast, then perform the 
obligatory prayer while riding your mount." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 456 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 465 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 172 • Tafsir al-Safi, Part 1, p. 269 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 442 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, 
p. 239 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 370 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1342

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.en:
In the narration from Zurarah from Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} who said: "One who 
fears robbers should perform their prayer by gesturing while on their mount”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V. 1, p. 466 • Al-Wafi, V. 8, p. 1074 • Tafsir al-Safi, Part 1, p. 270 
• Wasail Al-Shia, V. 8, p. 441 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Pt 1, p. 240 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 370 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1343

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.ar:
1345  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ى  صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلْمُّوَّإق َّق َّه ِ 
إ ِي مَّاءً عَّلَّى ذَّإن  َّي ِهِ ي َّصْن َّغُّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى إَّلن  ُّز ُّولِ ق َّالَّ  
«إَّلس ُّح ُّوذَّ إ َّج ْق َّض َّ مِن َّ إَّلز ُّكُّوعِ و 
لأَّ ي َّذُّورُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ وَّ لَّكِن ْ إ َّي ْن َّمَّا ذَّإرَّب ْ ذَّإن  َّي ُّهُّ ع َّي ْزَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ي َّسْي َّق ْن ِلُّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه َّ ن ِا َّو َّلِ ي َّكْن ِي زَّة ٍ جِي ن َّ ي َّي َّوَّج  َّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
t has been permitted, in the case of the prayer of fear from a wild beast when 
a person fears for their life, to simply say the takbir (proclaiming "Allahu Akbar") 
without making gestures.  
This was narrated by Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the Imams {a.s}. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V. 1, p. 466 • Al-Wafi, V8, p. 1075 • Wasail Al-Shia, V8, p. 441 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1344

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.ar:
1346  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عُّن َّي ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ف ِ عَّلَّى إَّلط  َّهْزِ إ ِي مَّاء   
ن ِزَّإ ْسِك َّ وَّ ي َّكْن ِي ز  وَّ إَّلْمُّسَّاي َّق َّه ِ ي َّكْن ِي ز  ن ِع َّي ْزِ إ ِي مَّاءٍ وَّ إَّلْمُّطَّارَّذَّة ِ إ ِي مَّاء  ي ُّ صَّلِّى  كُّل ُّ رَّج ُّلٍ عَّلَّى جِي َّالِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"One who fears robbers or a wild beast should perform the prayer of a stopped traveler 
using gestures while on their mount."  
I (Zurarah) asked: "What if the person does not have wudu (ablution) and cannot 
dismount? What should they do?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "They should perform tayammum (dry ablution) using the saddlecloth, 
saddle, or the cover of their mount, as it contains dust, and then pray, making the 
gesture for prostration lower than that of bowing. They do not need to turn to face the 
Qiblah; rather, they should face the Qiblah for the initial takbir (opening declaration) 
and then continue as the mount moves." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 466 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 173 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 8, p. 441 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1345
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.ar:
1347   -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ وَّ 
إَّلْعِس َّاءِ ق َّا َّمَّزَّهُّمْ ق َّكَّن  َّزُّوإ وَّ هَّل َّلُّوإ وَّ سَّن  َّحُّوإ رِج َّالأً وَّ رُّكْن َّان اً .
hadiths.en:
Ubaydullah ibn Ali Al -Halabi reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -
Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"The prayer during advancing combat (al -zahf) is performed on one's back with 
gestures of the head and the takbir (proclamation of 'Allahu Akbar'). During close 
fighting (al -musayafa), it involves takbir without gestures. During pursuit (al -
mutarada), it i s performed with gestures, and each person prays individually as per 
their situation."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 466 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 174 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 1069 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 443 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 86, p. 110 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1346
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ١٣٤٦

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.ar:
1348  -  وَّ ق ِى  كِي َّاب ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ِ إ َّن  َّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
إَّلي  َّكْن ِي زِ ي َّكْن ِي زَّي َّان ِ لِكُّلِّ صَّلاَّة ٍ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ لَّهَّا ي َّلاَّي اً مِن َّ إَّلي  َّكْن ِي زِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"The people missed the Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha prayers with Ali {a.s} on the day of 
Siffin. He instructed them to make the takbir (saying 'Allahu Akbar'), tahleel (saying 'La 
ilaha illAllah {SWT}'), and tasbih (glorifying Allah {SWT}) while standing and while mounted."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 467 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1069 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 444 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1347

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ١٣٤٧

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.ar:
1349   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مِهْزَّإن :  عَّن ْ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلْق ِي َّالِ ق َّق َّال 
كَّان ُّوإ وُّق ُّوق اً لأَّ ي َّق ْذِرُّون َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْح َّمَّاعَّه ِ ق َّالص َّلاَّة ُّ إ ِي مَّاء  .  
وإلعزي ان  ي صلى  ق اعذإ وي ص غ ي ذة على عوري ه، وإ ن  كان ث  إمزإ ة   وص عث  ي ذها على ق زج ها، ي م ي و مي ان  إ ي ماء وي كون  سح وذهما 
إ ج ق ض  من  ركوعهما، ولأ ي زكعان  ولأ ي سح ذإن  ق ي ن ذو ما ج لق هما ولكن  إ ي ماء ن زو وسهما . 
وإ ن  كان وإ ج ماعه  صلوإ وجذإن ا . وق ى  إلماء وإلطي ن  ي كون  إلصلاة  ن الأي ماء  وإلزكوع إ ج ق ض  من  إلسح وذ  .
hadiths.en:
In the book of Abdullah ibn al-Mughira, it is reported that Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The minimum that suffices for the prayer during close combat (al -musayafa) is two 
takbirs (proclamations of 'Allahu Akbar') for each prayer, except for the Maghrib 
prayer, for which there are three takbirs." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 467 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 174 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 8, p. 444 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1348
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ١٣٤٨

hadiths.chapter: 62. THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked him (Abu Abdullah {a.s}) about the prayer during 
battle.  
Imam {a.s} said: "When they meet and engage in fighting, the prayer at that time is 
performed with takbir (proclamation of 'Allahu Akbar'). If they are standing and unable 
to form a congregation, then the prayer is performed with gestures."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Regarding one who is unclothed, they should pray while seated and cover their private 
parts with their hands. If it is a woman, She should cover herself in a similar manner. 
They should perform gestures for the movements of the prayer, making their 
prostration lower than their bowing, and they should not bow or prostrate fully to avoid 
exposing themselves, instead using gestures with their heads.  
If they are a group, they should pray individually. When praying in water or mud, the 
prayer is performed using gestures, with the bowing made less deep than the 
prostration.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 458 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Vol. 1, p. 468 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1067 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 444

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 62, H. 1349

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF FEAR, PURSUIT, ENCOUNTER, AND

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1351  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ ق َّالَّ: ق َّالَّ لِى  إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ج ُّلُّ ي َّمِي ن َّهُّ ق َّلْي َّق ُّلْ 
ن ِسْمِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّسْلَّمْث ُّ ن َّق ْسِى  إ ِلَّي ْك َّ وَّ وَّج  َّهْث ُّ وَّج ْهِى  إ ِلَّي ْك َّ وَّ ق َّو َّص ْث ُّ  إ َّمْزِى  إ ِلَّي ْك َّ وَّ إ َّلْح َّا ْب ُّ ط َّهْزِى  إ ِلَّي ْك َّ وَّ  
ي َّوَّك َّلْث ُّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ رَّهْن َّه ً مِن ْك َّ وَّ رَّع ْن َّه ً إ ِلَّي ْك َّ لأَّ مَّلْح َّا َّ وَّ لأَّ مَّن ْح َّى مِن ْك َّ إ ِلأ َّ إ ِلَّي ْك َّ إ مَّن ْث ُّ ن ِكِي َّان ِ ك َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  إ َّن ْز َّلْث َّ وَّ ن ِزَّسُّولِك َّ إَّل َّذ ِى   
إ َّرْسَّلْث َّ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّسَّن ِّ حُّ ي َّسْن ِي حَّ ق َّاطِمَّه َّ إَّلز  َّهْزَّإءِ عَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ مَّن ْ إ َّصَّان َّهُّ ق َّز َّع  عِن ْذَّ مَّن َّامِهِ ق َّلْي َّق ْزَّإ ْ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّوَّ ى إ ِلَّى ق ِزَّإس ِهِ، 
إَّلْمُّعَّوِّذ َّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ إ ي َّه َّ إَّلْكُّزْسِى ِّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever purifies themselves (performs wudu) and then goes to their bed, their bed 
will be like their mosque (place of prayer). If they remember that they are not in a state 
of wudu, they should perform tayammum (dry ablution) using the fabric of their 
covering, whatever it may be, and as long as they remember Allah {SWT}, they will remain 
in a state of prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 469 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 116 • Makarem al-
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 288 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1581 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 1, p. 378 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 
182 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 308 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 157 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1350

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1352 - وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  ي َّق ُّولَّ عِن ْذَّ مَّن َّامِهِ: 
إ ُّعِي ذ ُّ ن َّق ْسِى  وَّ ذ ُّرِّي  َّي ِى  وَّ إ َّهْلَّ ن َّي ْي ِى  وَّ مَّالِى  ن ِكَّلِمَّاب ِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلي  َّام َّاب ِ مِن ْ كُّلِّ س َّي ْطَّان ٍ وَّ هَّام َّه ٍ  وَّ مِن ْ كُّلِّ عَّي ْن ٍ لأَّم َّه ٍ ق َّذ َّلِك َّ  
إَّل َّذ ِى  عَّو َّذ َّ ن ِهِ ج َّن ْزَّن ِي لُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ، إَّلْحَّسَّن َّ وَّ إَّلْحُّسَّ ي ْن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ .
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala report s from Muhammad ibn Muslim that Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} said to 
me: "When a man places his right side (for sleep), he should say:  
'In the Name of Allah {SWT}. O’ Allah {SWT}, I have submitted my soul to You, turned my 
face towards You, entrusted my affairs to You, and placed my reliance upon You, in 
fear of You and in hope of You. There is no refuge and no escape from You except to 
You. I believe in Your Book, which You have revealed, and in Your Messenger, whom 
You have sent.'  
Then he should recite the Tasbih of Sayyida Fatimah Al -Zahra {S.A}.  
If someone experiences fear at bedtime, they should recite the two protective 
chapters (al-Mu'awwidhatayn) and Ayat al-Kursi (The Verse of the Throne)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 469 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 116 • Makarem al-
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 288 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1351

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1353  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ لَّه هُّ إ َّجَّذ   و
إَّلْكٰاق ِزُّون  عِن ْذَّ مَّن َّامِك َّ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا ن َّزَّإءَّة   مِن َّ إَّلس ِّزْك ِ وَّ « ن ِسْن َّه ُّ إَّلز َّب ِّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل .
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala' reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams {a.s}, 
who said: "A man should not neglect to say at bedtime:  
'I seek protection for myself, my descendants, my family, and my wealth with the 
complete words of Allah {SWT} from every devil, harmful creature, and every envious 
eye.' This is what Archangel Jibril {a.s} used to protect Hasan and Hussain {a.s}." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 470 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 116 • Makarem al-
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 288 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1583 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 447 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 
196 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 174 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1352

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan reports from Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"Recite 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) and ‘Qul ya-ayyuhal kaafirun’ (Surah 
al-Kafirun) before you sleep, for they are a declaration of freedom from shirk 
(associating partners with Allah {SWT}), and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad'  (Surah Al -Ikhlas) 
affirms the nature of the Lord {AZJ}, Mighty and Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 470 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 116 • Makarem al-
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 288 • Al -Wafi, V. 9, p. 1585 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 6, p. 450 • Bihar Al -Anwar, V. 73, p. 195 • 
Bihar Al Anwar, V. 84, p. 174 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Pt 5, p. 688 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 14, p. 472 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1353

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1355  -  وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «  ن َّس َّز  مِي ْلُّكُّمْ ي ُّوجىٰ إ ِلَّى  َّ إ َّن  َّمٰا 
إ ِلٰهُّكُّمْ إ ِلٰه  وٰإجِذ   إ ِلَّى إ ج ِزِهَّا سَّطَّغَّ لَّهُّ ن ُّور  إ ِلَّى إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ إَّلْحَّزَّإمِ جَّس ْوُّ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلن  ُّورِ مَّلاَّن ِكَّه   ي َّسْي َّع ْق ِزُّون َّ لَّهُّ جَّي  َّ ى ي ُّصْن ِحَّ .
hadiths.en:
Bakr ibn Muhammad reported that Imam {a.s} said:  
"Whoever says the following three times when taking their place to sleep:  
'All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, Who is exalted and dominates. All praise is due to Allah 
{SWT}, Who is hidden yet knows. All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, Who rules and controls 
with power. All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, Who gives life to dead and causes living to 
die, and is over all things capable,' will be free of sins as on the day their mother gave 
birth to them." 
[REFERENCES] Qurb al-Isnad, Part 1, p. 35 • Al -Kafi, Volume 2, p. 535 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, 
p. 470 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 153 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 117 • Makarem al-
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 289 • Meftah al -Falah, Part 1, p. 282 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1577 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 
448 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 196 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1354

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1356  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّامِزُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ ج ُّذ َّإعَّه َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ي َّق ْزَّإ ُّ إ ج ِزَّ إَّلْكَّهْف ِ  
جِي ن َّ ي َّن َّامُّ إ ِلأ َّ إِسْي َّي ْق َّظ َّ مِن ْ مَّن َّامِهِ ق ِى  إَّلس َّاعَّه ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ي ُّزِي ذُّ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} said: "Whoever recites this verse before sleeping:  
'Say, I am only a man like you, to whom it has been revealed that your God is One God' 
(Surah al-Kahf 18:110) until the end of the verse, a light will shine for him to the Sacred 
Mosque (Masjid al -Haram), filled with angels who seek forgiveness for him until he 
awakens”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 470 • Uddat al -Da’i, Part 1, p. 301 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 
1585 • Tafsir al-Safi, Part 3, p. 270 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 3, p. 609 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 316 
• Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 8, p. 183 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1355

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1357  -  وَّ رَّوَّى سَّعْذ  إَّلْأ ِسْكَّاف ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال «
ي ُّصِي ن َّهُّ عَّق ْزَّب   وَّ لأَّ هَّام َّه   جَّي  َّى ي ُّصْن ِحَّ: إ َّعُّوذ ُّ ن ِكَّلِمَّاب ِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلي  َّام َّاب ِ إَّل َّي ِى  لأَّ ي ُّح َّاوِر ُّهُّن  َّ ن ز   وَّ لأَّ ق َّاج ِز  مِن ْ س َّزِّ مَّا ذ َّرَّإ َّ وَّ 
مِن ْ س َّزِّ مَّا ن َّزَّإ َّ وَّ مِن ْ  س َّزِّ كُّلِّ ذَّإن  َّه ٍ .
hadiths.en:
Amir ibn Abdullah ibn Juza'ah narrated from Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"There is no servant who recites the end of Surah al -Kahf before sleeping except that 
they will wake up at the hour they desire." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 471 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 175 • Meftah al -
Falah, Part 1, p. 283 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 229 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol. 3, p. 610 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1356
reference.chapter: WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ١٣٥٦

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1358  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ق ِى  ق ِزَّإس ِك َّ: إَّلل َّهُّم َّ 
إ ِن ِّى  إ َّعُّوذ ُّ ن ِك َّ مِن َّ إَّلأِجْي ِلاَّمِ وَّ مِن ْ سُّوءِ إَّلْأ َّجْلاَّمِ وَّ مِن ْ إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّلاَّعَّث َّ ن ِى َّ إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ُّ ق ِى  إَّلْي َّق َّط َّه ِ وَّ إَّلْمَّن َّامِ .
hadiths.en:
Sa'd al-Iskaf reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"Whoever recites these words, I guarantee that no scorpion or harmful creature will 
harm them until morning: 'I seek refuge with the complete words of Allah {SWT}, which 
neither the righteous nor the wicked can surpass, from the evil of what He created, 
from the evil of what He brought forth, and from the evil of every creature over which 
He holds control. Truly, my Lord {AZJ} is on a straight path.'" 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 2, p. 570 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 471 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 117 • Makarem al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 290 • Al-Masbah (Lalkafami) Vol. 1, p. 222 • Meftah al-Falah, 
Part 1, p. 283 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1647 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 449 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 92, p. 144 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1357

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ١٣٥٧

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.ar:
1359  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّن  َّاسُّ ن ْن ُّ هِلاَّلٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال جَّذ  ق َّظ ُّ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّرَّإذَّ 
إ َّن ْ ي َّن َّام إَّلس َّمٰاوٰإب ِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّز ُّولأٰ وَّ لَّن ِن ْ ر ٰإلَّي ٰا إ ِن ْ إ َّمْسَّكَّهُّمٰا مِن ْ إ َّجَّذٍ مِن ْ ن َّعْذِةِ إ ِن  َّهُّ كٰان َّ جَّلِ ي ماً  
ع َّق ُّورإً ق َّسَّق َّظَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْن َّي ْث ُّ .
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} 
who said:  
"If you fear experiencing a state of janabah (ritual impurity) during sleep, say in your 
bed:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, I seek refuge with You from experiencing a wet dream, from evil dreams, 
and from being manipulated by Shaytan (the devil) in wakefulness and in sleep.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 471 • Makarem al -Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 290 • Meftah al -
Falah, Part 1, p. 284 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1578 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 448 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1358

hadiths.chapter: 63. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY WHEN THEY LIE DOWN
hadiths.en:
Al-‘Abbas ibn Hilal reported from Abu al -Hasan al-Ridha {a.s}, from his father 
{a.s} who said:  
"No one has ever recited, when intending to sleep :  
'Indeed, Allah {SWT} holds the heavens and the earth lest they cease, and if they should 
cease, no one could hold them [in place] after Him. Indeed, He is Forbearing and 
Forgiving' (Qur'an 35:41) without being protected, such that even if a house were to 
collapse upon him, he would be safe." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 471 • Thawab al-
A’mal wa Iqab al -A’mal, Part 1, p. 152 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 117 • Falah Al -Sael, Part 1, p. 281 • 
Meftah al-Falah, Part 1, p. 284 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1586  • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 4, p. 534 • Bihar Al Anwar, 
Vol. 73, p. 201 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 211 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 10, p. 581

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 63, H. 1359

hadiths.chapter: 64. THE REWARD OF THE NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1361  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ن َّحْز  إَّلس َّق  َّاءُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
وَّ إ ِق ْطَّارَّ إَّلص َّان ِمِ وَّ لِق َّاءَّ إَّلْأ ِج ْوَّإن ِ .
hadiths.en:
Archangel Jibril {a.s} descended upon the Prophet {saws} and said to Him {saws}:  
"O’ Muhammad {saws+, live as long as you wish, for you will surely die; love whomever 
you wish, for you will be separated from them; and do whatever you wish, for you will 
be recompensed for it. The honor of a believer lies in their night prayer, and their dignity 
is in refraining from harming people”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 471 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4, p. 399 • Al -
Waezin, Vol. 2, p. 321 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 101 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 64, H. 1360
reference.chapter: THE REWARD OF THE NIGHT PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE REWARD OF THE NIGHT PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ١٣٦٠

hadiths.chapter: 64. THE REWARD OF THE NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1362 - وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلْأ َّو َّلُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل 
إِن ْي ِع ٰاءَّ رِص ْوٰإن ِ إَّللّٰهِ  ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Bahr al-Saqqa' reported from Imam Jafar Al-Sadiq {a.s} Ali who said:  
"Indeed, among the blessings and comfort from Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, 
are three things: praying in the late hours of the night (Tahajjud), breaking the fast (iftar) 
of a fasting person, and meeting with one's brothers (fellow believers)."  
[REFERENCES] Da‘ā’im al-Islām, Part 1, p. 271 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 472 • Amali (by Al -
Tusi) Vol. 1, p. 172 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 102 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 153 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 14, p. 587 • 
Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 71, p. 353 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 143 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 258 • Mustadrak 
Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 327 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 64, H. 1361
